expectations.text: Produces a multi-level directory map showing the folder structure with annotations
expectations.evidence: Full annotated tree output covering 3 levels with per-folder descriptions (e.g. '← Old agent configs (DonDog, Chef setup packages)')

expectations.text: Organizes archive candidates into priority tiers (not just a flat list), explaining the signal/reason for each
expectations.evidence: Three tiers: Tier 1 (already archived), Tier 2 (strong candidates with reason column), Tier 3 (cleanup/consolidation). Each row has a 'Signal' or 'Detail' column.

expectations.text: Handles the case where no files are 90+ days old gracefully (uses content signals instead of just timestamp filtering)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 'The find command returned 0 such files, meaning everything has been touched in the last 90 days — likely due to macOS propagating timestamps on directory access. Instead, archive candidates are identified by content signals.'

expectations.text: Identifies and calls out empty directories (anna/, Donjon_Images/, tasks/, Private & Shared/) as cleanup candidates
expectations.evidence: Tier 2 archive candidates include 'Private & Shared/ (empty) — delete', 'Donjon_Images/ (empty) — delete', 'summaries/D.D. Heartbeat Orchestrator/ (empty) — delete', 'tasks/ (empty) — delete', 'anna/ (empty) — delete'

expectations.text: Identifies the Jacob-facing PDFs and ZIPs at root as deliverable archive candidates
expectations.evidence: Tier 2 explicitly lists Donjonatworkfinal.zip ('final' = delivered), donjonatwork-editorsreport.zip, 'Donjon at Work.pdf', 'Donjon at Work copy for Jacob.pdf' ('copy for Jacob' = sent), 'forjacob.pdf' ('for Jacob' = sent)

expectations.text: Generates or includes INDEX.md-style content (structured map with annotations per folder)
expectations.evidence: Full directory map section with tree + annotations, counts table, and archive recommendations — equivalent to INDEX.md content even if not saved to file (skill phase only asked for audit, not full INDEX write)
